name: Cotton Poplin Relaxed Shirt
description: A relaxed poplin shirt with clean lines and no unnecessary detail — a formal option that doesn't feel stiff.
whyThisFitsMyArchive: White is my go-to formal alternative to a full suit. The relaxed cut means it works buttoned up for something serious or open over a tee for something casual.
whatItReplaces: Replaces the pleated formal shirt I used to think I needed for 'serious' occasions. This does the same job in something I'd actually reach for on a random Tuesday, not just for a gallery opening.
whyIChooseThis: Over slim-fit dress shirts: the relaxed cut is more forgiving and more comfortable through a full day. Over statement fabrics: plain poplin goes with everything I own.
whatItPairsWithInMyCollection: White with the wool trousers for anything formal. Layered under the overshirt or bomber for daily wear.
layeringCombinations: Alone, buttoned, for formal. Open over a tee for casual. Under the overshirt on cooler days.
colourCombinations: White is the formal default. Black and stone cover casual. Navy gets the least wear but earns its place for variety.
bestOccasions: Formal-adjacent occasions that don't require a full suit
costPerWearReasoning: ₹5,990, and it's the shirt I reach for whenever something needs to look considered without being stiff — closer to weekly wear than occasional.
whatMakesItTimeless: Plain poplin, clean construction, a fit that isn't chasing a trend cycle.
whatProblemItSolves: A shirt that moves between formal and casual without needing a separate wardrobe for each.
whatCouldReplaceIt: A dress shirt if I need something stiffer, but this covers most situations already.
layeringOver: a plain tee if left open
layeringUnder: the overshirt or bomber on cooler days
alternativeUnder3000: Uniqlo Extra Fine Cotton Broadcloth Shirt (~₹1,990 at uniqlo.com/in)
alternativeUnder10000: COS Poplin Shirt (~₹5,000-₹7,000 import via cos.com)
buyRecommendation: Wait for Sale — MD poplin drops to ~₹2,800-₹3,500 at clearance, which is the right price.
